description: The duration in days since a user's last login, indicating how long they have been inactive.
definition: $\text{Inactivity (days)} = \text{CurrentDate} - \text{LastLoginDate}$
Calculates the number of days passed since the user last logged in.

description: The age of a user's account in days since its creation.
definition: $\text{AccountAge (days)} = \text{CurrentDate} - \text{CreationDate}$
Measures the total duration, in days, that a user account has existed.

description: The average duration of a user's sessions in seconds.
definition: $\bar{x} = \frac{\sum_{i=1}^{n} \text{SessionDuration}_i}{n}$
The mean duration, in seconds, of all recorded user sessions.

description: The average number of characters in the content of posts created by a user.
definition: $\bar{L} = \frac{\sum \text{LENGTH}(\text{PostContent})}{\text{COUNT}(\text{Posts})}$
Calculates the average character length of a user's posts.

description: The total number of posts created by a user.
definition: $N_{\text{posts}} = \sum_{i=1}^{n} 1$
A simple count of all posts associated with a specific user ID.

description: The total number of comments made by a user.
definition: $N_{\text{comments}} = \sum_{i=1}^{n} 1$
A simple count of all comments associated with a specific user ID.

description: The ratio of likes to views for a given post, indicating engagement efficiency.
definition: $R_{\text{like/view}} = \frac{\text{LikesCount}}{\text{ViewsCount}}$
The number of likes a post receives divided by its total number of views.

description: The ratio of comments to views for a given post, indicating discussion generation.
definition: $R_{\text{comment/view}} = \frac{\text{CommentsCount}}{\text{ViewsCount}}$
The number of comments a post receives divided by its total number of views.

description: The age of a post in days since it was published.
definition: $\text{PostAge (days)} = \text{CurrentDate} - \text{PublishedDate}$
Measures the total duration, in days, since a post was published.

description: The percentage of notifications that a user has marked as read.
definition: $\text{ReadRate} = \frac{\sum \text{NotificationsRead}}{\text{TotalNotifications}}$
The ratio of read notifications to the total number of notifications for a user.

description: The average time taken to resolve a report, in hours.
definition: $T_{\text{avg_resolve}} = \text{AVG}(\text{ResolutionTimestamp} - \text{ReportTimestamp})$
The average duration, in hours, from when a report is submitted to when it is resolved.

description: The total number of reports that are currently not resolved.
definition: $N_{\text{unresolved}} = \sum 1 \text{ where is_resolved} = \text{false}$
A count of all reports where the `is_resolved` flag is false.

description: The average number of tags applied to each post.
definition: $\text{AvgTags} = \frac{\sum \text{Tags}}{\sum \text{Posts}}$
The total number of tag associations divided by the total number of unique posts.

description: The sum of file sizes for all media uploads, in megabytes.
definition: $\text{Storage (MB)} = \frac{\sum \text{FileSize}}{1024^2}$
The total size of all uploaded media files, converted to megabytes.

description: The ratio of user activities performed on mobile devices versus desktop devices.
definition: $R_{\text{mobile/desktop}} = \frac{\sum \text{MobileActivities}}{\sum \text{DesktopActivities}}$
The count of activities from mobile operating systems divided by the count from desktop operating systems.

description: The average time it takes for a recipient to read a private message after it's sent, in minutes.
definition: $L_{\text{avg_read}} = \text{AVG}(\text{ReadTimestamp} - \text{SendingTime})$
The average duration, in minutes, between a message being sent and it being read.

description: The total number of users following a specific user.
definition: $N_{\text{followers}} = \sum 1 \text{ where following_id} = \text{user_id}$
Counts the number of rows in the followers table for a given user being followed.

description: The average number of likes received on posts created by a specific user.
definition: $\bar{L} = \frac{\sum \text{LikesCount}}{\text{TotalPosts}}$
The average of `likes_count` for all posts by a specific user.

description: The ratio of dislikes to total reactions (likes + dislikes) on a post.
definition: $R_{\text{neg}} = \frac{\text{Dislikes}}{\text{Likes} + \text{Dislikes}}$
Measures the proportion of negative reactions out of all positive and negative reactions on a post.

description: The average number of posts created per day across the entire platform.
definition: $V_{\text{post}} = \frac{\text{TotalPosts}}{\text{UniqueDays}}$
The total number of posts divided by the number of distinct days on which posts were created.

description: A user is considered active if their last login was within the last 30 days.
definition: $\text{User is Active if } (\text{CurrentDate} - \text{LastLoginDate}) \le 30 \text{ days}$
A classification where users are marked as active if they have logged in within the past 30 days.

description: A user is classified as a power user if they are in the top 10% for combined posts and comments.
definition: $\text{User is Power User if } \text{Rank}(N_{\text{posts}} + N_{\text{comments}}) \in \text{Top } 10\%$
A classification for users whose total number of posts and comments places them in the 90th percentile or higher.

description: A classification of user profile completeness based on the presence of bio, website, and avatar.
definition: $\text{Level} = f(\text{bio}, \text{website}, \text{avatar})$
Categorizes a profile as 'Complete', 'Partial', or 'Incomplete' based on whether the bio, website, and avatar fields are filled.

description: Content is considered stale if it has not been updated in the last 365 days.
definition: $\text{Content is Stale if } (\text{CurrentDate} - \text{UpdateDate}) > 365 \text{ days}$
A classification for posts that have not been modified for more than one year.

description: A post is considered viral if it achieves over 100,000 views, 5,000 likes, and 1,000 shares.
definition: $\text{Viral if } (V > 100k) \land (L > 5k) \land (S > 1k)$
A set of criteria where a post is defined as viral if its views, likes, and shares exceed specific high thresholds.

description: A post is flagged as controversial if the dislike-to-like ratio is over 0.5 and it has more than 50 comments.
definition: $\text{Controversial if } (\frac{\text{Dislikes}}{\text{Likes}} > 0.5) \land (\text{Comments} > 50)$
A flag for posts with a high ratio of dislikes to likes and a significant number of comments, indicating debate or disagreement.

description: A post is classified as high-engagement if the sum of its likes, comments, and shares exceeds 1000.
definition: $\text{High Engagement if } (L + C + S) > 1000$
A classification for posts where the total number of interactions (likes, comments, shares) is greater than 1000.

description: A report is considered high severity if its severity level is 3 or greater.
definition: $\text{High Severity if } \text{SeverityLevel} \ge 3$
A classification for reports that are marked with the highest level of severity, requiring immediate attention.

description: A user is flagged as frequently reported if they have been the subject of 5 or more unique reports.
definition: $\text{Frequently Reported if } \sum \text{Reports} \ge 5$
A flag for users who have been reported by others five or more times.

description: A tag is considered unused if its usage count is zero.
definition: $\text{Unused Tag if } \text{UsageCount} = 0$
A classification for tags that are not associated with any posts.

description: A category is considered orphaned if it contains zero posts or items.
definition: $\text{Orphaned Category if } \text{ItemCount} = 0$
A classification for categories that have no associated content.

description: Classifies a user's message responsiveness as 'High', 'Medium', or 'Low' based on average read latency.
definition: $\text{Responsiveness} = f(L_{\text{avg_read}})$
Categorizes users based on their average message read time: High (<1hr), Medium (1-24hrs), or Low (>24hrs).

description: Content is eligible to be featured if it is less than 7 days old and has over 1000 likes and 10000 views.
definition: $\text{Featured if } (\text{Age} < 7\text{d}) \land (L > 1k) \land (V > 10k)$
A set of rules for promoting content, based on recent publication and high engagement metrics.

description: A comment is flagged as potential spam if it contains 'http' and is from an IP address with more than 5 comments in an hour.
definition: $\text{Spam if } ('http' \in \text{Content}) \land (N_{\text{comments from IP in 1hr}} > 5)$
Defines a pattern for identifying spam based on the presence of links and high-frequency posting from a single IP.

description: Measures the number of distinct permissions assigned to a user role.
definition: $\text{Granularity} = \text{COUNT}(\text{Permissions})$
A count of the individual permission keys within the role's JSON permissions object.

description: A user is considered dormant if their last login was more than 180 days ago and their account is older than 365 days.
definition: $\text{Dormant if } (\text{Inactivity} > 180\text{d}) \land (\text{Age} > 365\text{d})$
A classification for established users who have been inactive for an extended period.

description: A user is influential if they have over 1000 followers and their posts average over 100 likes.
definition: $\text{Influential if } (N_{\text{followers}} > 1k) \land (\bar{L}_{\text{posts}} > 100)$
Defines an influential user based on having both a large audience (followers) and high-quality engagement (likes).

description: Classifies a comment thread's depth as 'Flat' (0 replies), 'Shallow' (1-2 replies), or 'Deep' (>2 replies).
definition: $\text{Depth} = f(N_{\text{replies}})$
Categorizes comment threads based on the number of direct replies to the parent comment.

description: Identifies notification types, such as 'mention' or 'private_message', that are considered high priority.
definition: $\text{High Priority if } \text{Type} \in \{\text{'mention', 'private_message'}\}$
A filter to select notifications that are user-specific and require direct attention.

description: Categorizes users as 'Daily', 'Weekly', or 'Monthly' active based on the frequency of their activities.
definition: $\text{Frequency} = f(\frac{\sum \text{Activities}}{\Delta t})$
Classifies users based on their average number of activities per day into frequency brackets.

description: Defines the criteria for a report's verification status (e.g., 'verified', 'unverified', 'pending').
definition: $\text{Status} \in \{\text{'verified', 'unverified', 'pending'}\}$
A classification system for the states a report can be in during its lifecycle.

description: Categorizes media into types such as 'Image', 'Video', 'Audio', or 'Document' based on file extension or type field.
definition: $\text{Category} = f(\text{MediaType})$
A system for grouping different media formats into broader categories.

description: Flags activity as potential bot behavior if many actions originate from the same IP in a very short time frame without a standard user agent.
definition: $\text{Bot if } (N_{\text{actions from IP in 1min}} > 10) \land (\text{UserAgent is non-standard})$
A heuristic to identify automated behavior based on high frequency of actions from a single IP combined with an unusual user agent.

description: The current workflow status of a post (e.g., 'Draft', 'Published', 'Archived').
definition: $\text{Status} = \text{lookup}(\text{status_id})$
Retrieves the human-readable name of a post's current status from the status table.

description: Categorizes a user's reputation based on a combination of their average post likes and the number of times they've been reported.
definition: $\text{Reputation} = f(\bar{L}_{\text{posts}}, N_{\text{reports}})$
A classification system ('Excellent', 'Good', 'Needs Review') based on a user's positive contributions versus negative reports.

description: A specific user who has not logged in for more than 90 days.
definition: $\text{Condition}: \text{Inactivity} > 90 \text{ days}$
An example of a specific user who meets the criteria for being inactive, having not logged in for over three months.

description: A specific post that meets the criteria for being viral (e.g., > 100,000 views).
definition: $\text{Condition}: \text{Views} > 100,000$
A specific instance of a post that has achieved a very high number of views, qualifying it as viral.

description: A specific report that is marked with the highest severity level.
definition: $\text{Condition}: \text{SeverityLevel} \ge 3$
An example of a specific report that requires immediate moderator attention due to its high severity.

description: A specific tag that is among the top 5 most used tags on the platform.
definition: $\text{Condition}: \text{Rank}(\text{UsageCount}) \le 5$
An illustration of a tag that is frequently applied to posts, indicating a popular topic.

knowledge: Example of a Large Media File
description: A specific media file that is larger than 100MB.
definition: $\text{Condition}: \text{FileSize} > 100 \text{ MB}$
A specific instance of an uploaded file that consumes a significant amount of storage space.

description: A composite score indicating a user's overall engagement, calculated from their posts, comments, and followers.
definition: $\text{Score} = 2 \cdot N_{\text{posts}} + 1 \cdot N_{\text{comments}} + 3 \cdot N_{\text{followers}}$
A weighted sum of a user's total posts, comments, and followers to quantify their platform engagement.

description: A score measuring an author's influence based on their average post likes and follower count.
definition: $\text{Score} = 0.7 \cdot \bar{L}_{\text{posts}} + 0.3 \cdot N_{\text{followers}}$
A metric that combines audience size (followers) with audience appreciation (likes) to rank author influence.

description: A score assessing post quality based on positive engagement ratios minus a negativity penalty.
definition: $\text{Score} = 0.6 \cdot R_{\text{like/view}} + 0.4 \cdot R_{\text{comment/view}} - R_{\text{neg}}$
A measure of content performance that rewards likes and comments relative to views, while penalizing a high dislike ratio.

description: A high-level index of platform health combining the report resolution rate and notification read rate.
definition: $\text{Index} = \frac{(\text{Rate}_{\text{resolve}} + \text{Rate}_{\text{read}})}{2}$
An overall health score averaging the efficiency of moderation (resolved reports) and user engagement with the platform (read notifications).

description: A score that estimates the likelihood of a user churning, based on inactivity period and account age.
definition: $\text{Risk} = 0.8 \cdot \text{Inactivity} - 0.2 \cdot \text{AccountAge}$
Calculates churn risk where longer inactivity increases risk, and longer account tenure slightly decreases it.

description: An indicator of the overall negativity in the community, based on report counts and negativity ratios.
definition: $\text{Toxicity} = \%_{\text{posts reported}} + \bar{R}_{\text{neg}}$
A metric combining the prevalence of reported content with the average negativity of post reactions to gauge community health.

description: A predictive score of a post's potential to go viral, based on early engagement-to-view ratios.
definition: $\text{Potential} = 100 \cdot R_{\text{like/view}} + 150 \cdot R_{\text{comment/view}}$
A weighted score that emphasizes the comment-to-view ratio as a strong indicator of virality.

description: A score from 0 to 1 indicating user trustworthiness, penalized by how often they are reported.
definition: $\text{Trust} = 1 - \frac{N_{\text{reports against user}}}{N_{\text{posts}}}$
A reputation metric that starts at 1 and decreases based on the ratio of reports against the user to their total contributions.

description: A combined index of user activity based on session time, posting, and commenting.
definition: $\text{Index} = \frac{\bar{t}_{\text{session}}}{60} + N_{\text{posts}} + 0.5 \cdot N_{\text{comments}}$
A metric that quantifies user activity by summing their average session time in minutes with their content contributions.

description: A score from 0 to 1 indicating content freshness, which decays over time.
definition: $\text{Freshness} = \frac{1}{1 + \text{PostAge}_{\text{days}}}$
A decaying score where newer posts are closer to 1 and older posts approach 0.

description: Measures the health of the tagging system as the percentage of tags that are actively used.
definition: $\text{Health} = \frac{N_{\text{used tags}}}{N_{\text{total tags}}}$
The proportion of tags with a usage count greater than zero, indicating an efficient folksonomy.

description: A score reflecting the speed and completion rate of the moderation team.
definition: $\text{Efficiency} = \frac{1}{T_{\text{avg_resolve}}} \times \text{Rate}_{\text{resolve}}$
A metric that rewards both fast resolution times and a high percentage of resolved reports.

description: Measures a user's integration into the social graph through followers and direct messaging activity.
definition: $\text{Score} = N_{\text{followers}} + N_{\text{conversations}}$
A simple sum of a user's followers and the number of unique private message conversations they are part of.

description: A measure of a post's overall impact, combining its popularity with its freshness.
definition: $\text{Impact} = (0.5V + L + 1.5S) \times \text{Freshness}$
A metric where a post's weighted engagement score is multiplied by its freshness, giving more recent popular posts a higher impact.

description: A simple score representing a user's total content contributions.
definition: $\text{Score} = 2 \cdot N_{\text{posts}} + N_{\text{comments}}$
A weighted sum that values creating original posts twice as much as making comments.

description: A ratio of average session duration to user inactivity, indicating how engaging the platform is for active users.
definition: $\text{Stickiness} = \frac{\bar{t}_{\text{session}}}{\text{Inactivity}_{\text{days}}}$
A metric where a higher value suggests that when users do log in, they stay for a long time, indicating a 'sticky' platform.

description: A composite index reflecting a post's current value, combining raw popularity with its freshness.
definition: $\text{Value} = \text{Popularity} \times \text{Freshness}$
Multiplies a post's weighted popularity by its freshness score to determine its current relevance and value.

description: A comprehensive reputation score based on influence, penalized by the rate at which the user is reported.
definition: $\text{Reputation} = \text{Influence} \times \text{Trust}$
Combines a user's positive influence with their trustworthiness to create a holistic reputation metric.

description: The average number of likes and comments on posts within a specific category.
definition: $\text{Rate} = \text{AVG}(L+C) \text{ where category_id} = C$
Calculates the average total engagement (likes plus comments) for all posts belonging to a given category.

description: An indicator of the current load on the system, combining daily post velocity with total media storage.
definition: $\text{Load} = 0.1 \cdot V_{\text{post}} + 0.9 \cdot \text{Storage}_{\text{GB}}$
A metric where system load is primarily determined by total storage used, with a smaller contribution from the number of new posts.
